verses.latin_vulgate: Et convocatis duodecim discipulis suis, dedit illis potestatem spirituum immundorum, ut ejicerent eos, et curarent omnem languorem, et omnem infirmitatem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος τοὺς δώδεκα μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ ἔδωκεν αὐτοῖς ἐξουσίαν πνευμάτων ἀκαθάρτων ὥστε ἐκβάλλειν αὐτὰ καὶ θεραπεύειν πᾶσαν νόσον καὶ πᾶσαν μαλακίαν
verses.king_james: And when he had called unto *him* his twelve disciples, he gave them power *against* unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal all manner of sickness and all manner of disease.

verses.latin_vulgate: Duodecim autem Apostolorum nomina sunt hæc. Primus, Simon, qui dicitur Petrus: et Andreas frater ejus, Jacobus Zebedæi, et Joannes frater ejus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῶν δὲ δώδεκα ἀποστόλων τὰ ὀνόματά ἐστιν ταῦτα πρῶτος σίμων ὁ λεγόμενος πέτρος καὶ ἀνδρέας ὁ ἀδελφὸς αὐτοῦ ἰάκωβος ὁ τοῦ ζεβεδαίου καὶ ἰωάννης ὁ ἀδελφὸς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Now the names of the twelve apostles are these; The first, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother; James *the son* of Zebedee, and John his brother;

verses.latin_vulgate: Philippus, et Bartholomæus, Thomas, et Matthæus publicanus, Jacobus Alphæi, et Thaddæus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: φίλιππος καὶ βαρθολομαῖος φωμᾶς καὶ ματθαῖος ὁ τελώνης ἰάκωβος ὁ τοῦ ἁλφαίου καὶ λεββαῖος ὁ ἐπικληθεὶς φαδδαῖος
verses.king_james: Philip, and Bartholomew; Thomas, and Matthew the publican; James *the son* of Alphaeus, and Lebbaeus, whose surname was Thaddaeus;

verses.latin_vulgate: Simon Chananæus, et Judas Iscariotes, qui et tradidit eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σίμων ὁ κανανίτης καὶ ἰούδας ἰσκαριώτης ὁ καὶ παραδοὺς αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Simon the Canaanite, and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hos duodecim misit Jesus, præcipiens eis, dicens: In viam gentium ne abieritis, et in civitates Samaritanorum ne intraveritis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τούτους τοὺς δώδεκα ἀπέστειλεν ὁ ἰησοῦς παραγγείλας αὐτοῖς λέγων εἰς ὁδὸν ἐθνῶν μὴ ἀπέλθητε καὶ εἰς πόλιν σαμαρειτῶν μὴ εἰσέλθητε
verses.king_james: These twelve Jesus sent forth, and commanded them, saying, Go not into the way of the Gentiles, and into *any* city of the Samaritans enter ye not:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed potius ite ad oves quæ perierunt domus Israël.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πορεύεσθε δὲ μᾶλλον πρὸς τὰ πρόβατα τὰ ἀπολωλότα οἴκου ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Euntes autem prædicate, dicentes: Quia appropinquavit regnum cælorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πορευόμενοι δὲ κηρύσσετε λέγοντες ὅτι ἤγγικεν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν
verses.king_james: And as ye go, preach, saying, The kingdom of heaven is at hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Infirmos curate, mortuos suscitate, leprosos mundate, dæmones ejicite: gratis accepistis, gratis date.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσθενοῦντας θεραπεύετε λεπροὺς καθαρίζετε νεκροὺς ἐγείρετε δαιμόνια ἐκβάλλετε δωρεὰν ἐλάβετε δωρεὰν δότε
verses.king_james: Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, cast out devils: freely ye have received, freely give.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite possidere aurum, neque argentum, neque pecuniam in zonis vestris:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ κτήσησθε χρυσὸν μηδὲ ἄργυρον μηδὲ χαλκὸν εἰς τὰς ζώνας ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: Provide neither gold, nor silver, nor brass in your purses,

verses.latin_vulgate: non peram in via, neque duas tunicas, neque calceamenta, neque virgam: dignus enim est operarius cibo suo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ πήραν εἰς ὁδὸν μηδὲ δύο χιτῶνας μηδὲ ὑποδήματα μηδὲ ῥάβδον ἄξιος γὰρ ὁ ἐργάτης τῆς τροφῆς αὐτοῦ ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Nor scrip for *your* journey, neither two coats, neither shoes, nor yet staves: for the workman is worthy of his meat.

verses.latin_vulgate: In quamcumque autem civitatem aut castellum intraveritis, interrogate, quis in ea dignus sit: et ibi manete donec exeatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς ἣν δ᾽ ἂν πόλιν ἢ κώμην εἰσέλθητε ἐξετάσατε τίς ἐν αὐτῇ ἄξιός ἐστιν κἀκεῖ μείνατε ἕως ἂν ἐξέλθητε
verses.king_james: And into whatsoever city or town ye shall enter, enquire who in it is worthy; and there abide till ye go thence.

verses.latin_vulgate: Intrantes autem in domum, salutate eam, dicentes: Pax huic domui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰσερχόμενοι δὲ εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν ἀσπάσασθε αὐτήν
verses.king_james: And when ye come into an house, salute it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et siquidem fuerit domus illa digna, veniet pax vestra super eam: si autem non fuerit digna, pax vestra revertetur ad vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐὰν μὲν ᾖ ἡ οἰκία ἀξία ἐλθέτω ἡ εἰρήνη ὑμῶν ἐπ᾽ αὐτήν ἐὰν δὲ μὴ ᾖ ἀξία ἡ εἰρήνη ὑμῶν πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐπιστραφήτω
verses.king_james: And if the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it: but if it be not worthy, let your peace return to you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quicumque non receperit vos, neque audierit sermones vestros: exeunte foras de domo, vel civitate, excutite pulverem de pedibus vestris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὃς ἐὰν μὴ δέξηται ὑμᾶς μηδὲ ἀκούσῃ τοὺς λόγους ὑμῶν ἐξερχόμενοι τῆς οἰκίας ἢ τῆς πόλεως ἐκείνης ἐκτινάξατε τὸν κονιορτὸν τῶν ποδῶν ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, when ye depart out of that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amen dico vobis: Tolerabilius erit terræ Sodomorum et Gomorrhæorum in die judicii, quam illi civitati.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ἀνεκτότερον ἔσται γῇ σοδόμων καὶ γομόῤῥων ἐν ἡμέρᾳ κρίσεως ἢ τῇ πόλει ἐκείνῃ
verses.king_james: Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce ego mitto vos sicut oves in medio luporum. Estote ergo prudentes sicut serpentes, et simplices sicut columbæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδού ἐγὼ ἀποστέλλω ὑμᾶς ὡς πρόβατα ἐν μέσῳ λύκων γίνεσθε οὖν φρόνιμοι ὡς οἱ ὄφεις καὶ ἀκέραιοι ὡς αἱ περιστεραί
verses.king_james: Behold, I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves: be ye therefore wise as serpents, and harmless as doves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cavete autem ab hominibus. Tradent enim vos in conciliis, et in synagogis suis flagellabunt vos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσέχετε δὲ ἀπὸ τῶν ἀνθρώπων παραδώσουσιν γὰρ ὑμᾶς εἰς συνέδρια καὶ ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς αὐτῶν μαστιγώσουσιν ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: But beware of men: for they will deliver you up to the councils, and they will scourge you in their synagogues;

verses.latin_vulgate: et ad præsides, et ad reges ducemini propter me in testimonium illis, et gentibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπὶ ἡγεμόνας δὲ καὶ βασιλεῖς ἀχθήσεσθε ἕνεκεν ἐμοῦ εἰς μαρτύριον αὐτοῖς καὶ τοῖς ἔθνεσιν
verses.king_james: And ye shall be brought before governors and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them and the Gentiles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem tradent vos, nolite cogitare quomodo, aut quid loquamini: dabitur enim vobis in illa hora, quid loquamini:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ παραδιδῶσιν ὑμᾶς μὴ μεριμνήσητε πῶς ἢ τί λαλήσητε δοθήσεται γὰρ ὑμῖν ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ὥρᾳ τί λαλήσετε
verses.king_james: But when they deliver you up, take no thought how or what ye shall speak: for it shall be given you in that same hour what ye shall speak.

verses.latin_vulgate: non enim vos estis qui loquimini, sed Spiritus Patris vestri, qui loquitur in vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ ὑμεῖς ἐστε οἱ λαλοῦντες ἀλλὰ τὸ πνεῦμα τοῦ πατρὸς ὑμῶν τὸ λαλοῦν ἐν ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: For it is not ye that speak, but the Spirit of your Father which speaketh in you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tradet autem frater fratrem in mortem, et pater filium: et insurgent filii in parentes, et morte eos afficient:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παραδώσει δὲ ἀδελφὸς ἀδελφὸν εἰς θάνατον καὶ πατὴρ τέκνον καὶ ἐπαναστήσονται τέκνα ἐπὶ γονεῖς καὶ θανατώσουσιν αὐτούς
verses.king_james: And the brother shall deliver up the brother to death, and the father the child: and the children shall rise up against *their* parents, and cause them to be put to death.

verses.latin_vulgate: et eritis odio omnibus propter nomen meum: qui autem perseveraverit usque in finem, hic salvus erit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔσεσθε μισούμενοι ὑπὸ πάντων διὰ τὸ ὄνομά μου ὁ δὲ ὑπομείνας εἰς τέλος οὗτος σωθήσεται
verses.king_james: And ye shall be hated of all *men* for my name's sake: but he that endureth to the end shall be saved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem persequentur vos in civitate ista, fugite in aliam. Amen dico vobis, non consummabitis civitates Israël, donec veniat Filius hominis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ διώκωσιν ὑμᾶς ἐν τῇ πόλει ταύτῃ φεύγετε εἰς τὴν ἄλλην ἀμὴν γὰρ λέγω ὑμῖν οὐ μὴ τελέσητε τὰς πόλεις τοῦ ἰσραὴλ ἕως ἂν ἔλθῃ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου
verses.king_james: But when they persecute you in this city, flee ye into another: for verily I say unto you, Ye shall not have gone over the cities of Israel, till the Son of man be come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est discipulus super magistrum, nec servus super dominum suum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἔστιν μαθητὴς ὑπὲρ τὸν διδάσκαλον οὐδὲ δοῦλος ὑπὲρ τὸν κύριον αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: The disciple is not above *his* master, nor the servant above his lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: sufficit discipulo ut sit sicut magister ejus, et servo, sicut dominus ejus. Si patremfamilias Beelzebub vocaverunt, quanto magis domesticos ejus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀρκετὸν τῷ μαθητῇ ἵνα γένηται ὡς ὁ διδάσκαλος αὐτοῦ καὶ ὁ δοῦλος ὡς ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ εἰ τὸν οἰκοδεσπότην βεελζεβοὺλ ἐκάλεσαν πόσῳ μᾶλλον τοὺς οἰκιακοὺς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: It is enough for the disciple that he be as his master, and the servant as his lord. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebub, how much more *shall they call* them of his household?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne ergo timueritis eos. Nihil enim est opertum, quod non revelabitur: et occultum, quod non scietur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ οὖν φοβηθῆτε αὐτούς οὐδὲν γάρ ἐστιν κεκαλυμμένον ὃ οὐκ ἀποκαλυφθήσεται καὶ κρυπτὸν ὃ οὐ γνωσθήσεται
verses.king_james: Fear them not therefore: for there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; and hid, that shall not be known.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod dico vobis in tenebris, dicite in lumine: et quod in aure auditis, prædicate super tecta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃ λέγω ὑμῖν ἐν τῇ σκοτίᾳ εἴπατε ἐν τῷ φωτί καὶ ὃ εἰς τὸ οὖς ἀκούετε κηρύξατε ἐπὶ τῶν δωμάτων
verses.king_james: What I tell you in darkness, *that* speak ye in light: and what ye hear in the ear, *that* preach ye upon the housetops.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nolite timere eos qui occidunt corpus, animam autem non possunt occidere: sed potius timete eum, qui potest et animam et corpus perdere in gehennam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μὴ φοβηθῆτε ἀπὸ τῶν ἀποκτεινόντων τὸ σῶμα τὴν δὲ ψυχὴν μὴ δυναμένων ἀποκτεῖναι φοβηθήτε δὲ μᾶλλον τὸν δυνάμενον καὶ ψυχὴν καὶ σῶμα ἀπολέσαι ἐν γεέννῃ
verses.king_james: And fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nonne duo passeres asse veneunt? et unus ex illis non cadet super terram sine Patre vestro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχὶ δύο στρουθία ἀσσαρίου πωλεῖται καὶ ἓν ἐξ αὐτῶν οὐ πεσεῖται ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν ἄνευ τοῦ πατρὸς ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing? and one of them shall not fall on the ground without your Father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vestri autem capilli capitis omnes numerati sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμῶν δὲ καὶ αἱ τρίχες τῆς κεφαλῆς πᾶσαι ἠριθμημέναι εἰσίν
verses.king_james: But the very hairs of your head are all numbered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite ergo timere: multis passeribus meliores estis vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ οὖν φοβηθῆτε πολλῶν στρουθίων διαφέρετε ὑμεῖς
verses.king_james: Fear ye not therefore, ye are of more value than many sparrows.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis ergo qui confitebitur me coram hominibus, confitebor et ego eum coram Patre meo, qui in cælis est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς οὖν ὅστις ὁμολογήσει ἐν ἐμοὶ ἔμπροσθεν τῶν ἀνθρώπων ὁμολογήσω κἀγὼ ἐν αὐτῷ ἔμπροσθεν τοῦ πατρός μου τοῦ ἐν οὐρανοῖς
verses.king_james: Whosoever therefore shall confess me before men, him will I confess also before my Father which is in heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem negaverit me coram hominibus, negabo et ego eum coram Patre meo, qui in cælis est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅστις δ᾽ ἂν ἀρνήσηταί με ἔμπροσθεν τῶν ἀνθρώπων ἀρνήσομαι αὐτὸν κἀγὼ ἔμπροσθεν τοῦ πατρός μου τοῦ ἐν οὐρανοῖς
verses.king_james: But whosoever shall deny me before men, him will I also deny before my Father which is in heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite arbitrari quia pacem venerim mittere in terram: non veni pacem mittere, sed gladium:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ νομίσητε ὅτι ἦλθον βαλεῖν εἰρήνην ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν οὐκ ἦλθον βαλεῖν εἰρήνην ἀλλὰ μάχαιραν
verses.king_james: Think not that I am come to send peace on earth: I came not to send peace, but a sword.

verses.latin_vulgate: veni enim separare hominem adversus patrem suum, et filiam adversus matrem suam, et nurum adversus socrum suam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦλθον γὰρ διχάσαι ἄνθρωπον κατὰ τοῦ πατρὸς αὐτοῦ καὶ θυγατέρα κατὰ τῆς μητρὸς αὐτῆς καὶ νύμφην κατὰ τῆς πενθερᾶς αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: For I am come to set a man at variance against his father, and the daughter against her mother, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.

verses.latin_vulgate: et inimici hominis, domestici ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐχθροὶ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου οἱ οἰκιακοὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And a man's foes *shall be* they of his own household.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui aMatthew patrem aut matrem plus quam me, non est me dignus: et qui aMatthew filium aut filiam super me, non est me dignus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ φιλῶν πατέρα ἢ μητέρα ὑπὲρ ἐμὲ οὐκ ἔστιν μου ἄξιος καὶ ὁ φιλῶν υἱὸν ἢ θυγατέρα ὑπὲρ ἐμὲ οὐκ ἔστιν μου ἄξιος
verses.king_james: He that loveth father or mother more than me is not worthy of me: and he that loveth son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et qui non accipit crucem suam, et sequitur me, non est me dignus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὃς οὐ λαμβάνει τὸν σταυρὸν αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀκολουθεῖ ὀπίσω μου οὐκ ἔστιν μου ἄξιος
verses.king_james: And he that taketh not his cross, and followeth after me, is not worthy of me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui invenit animam suam, perdet illam: et qui perdiderit animam suam propter me, inveniet eam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ εὑρὼν τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ ἀπολέσει αὐτήν καὶ ὁ ἀπολέσας τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ ἕνεκεν ἐμοῦ εὑρήσει αὐτήν
verses.king_james: He that findeth his life shall lose it: and he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui recipit vos, me recipit: et qui me recipit, recipit eum qui me misit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δεχόμενος ὑμᾶς ἐμὲ δέχεται καὶ ὁ ἐμὲ δεχόμενος δέχεται τὸν ἀποστείλαντά με
verses.king_james: He that receiveth you receiveth me, and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui recipit prophetam in nomine prophetæ, mercedem prophetæ accipiet: et qui recipit justum in nomine justi, mercedem justi accipiet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δεχόμενος προφήτην εἰς ὄνομα προφήτου μισθὸν προφήτου λήψεται καὶ ὁ δεχόμενος δίκαιον εἰς ὄνομα δικαίου μισθὸν δικαίου λήψεται
verses.king_james: He that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet's reward; and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man's reward.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quicumque potum dederit uni ex minimis istis calicem aquæ frigidæ tantum in nomine discipuli: amen dico vobis, non perdet mercedem suam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὃς ἐὰν ποτίσῃ ἕνα τῶν μικρῶν τούτων ποτήριον ψυχροῦ μόνον εἰς ὄνομα μαθητοῦ ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν οὐ μὴ ἀπολέσῃ τὸν μισθὸν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And whosoever shall give to drink unto one of these little ones a cup of cold *water* only in the name of a disciple, verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise lose his reward.
